question: Design a `Book` class with private members for `title` (string), `author` (string), and `isbn` (string). Implement a public parameterized constructor to initialize these members and a public method `displayBookInfo()` to print all book details.

test_cases.input: The Lord of the Rings,J.R.R. Tolkien,978-0618053267
test_cases.expected:
Title: The Lord of the Rings
Author: J.R.R. Tolkien
ISBN: 978-0618053267

driver:
Book myBook("The Lord of the Rings", "J.R.R. Tolkien", "978-0618053267");
myBook.displayBookInfo();
errors: The `displayBookInfo()` method is not declared `const`, even though it does not modify the object's state. This is a semantic oversight in C++ class design, as it limits the method's use with const objects and doesn't clearly convey its intent.
